name: domain-expertise-template
description: Template for creating domain expertise skills. Copy and customize for your specific domain.
capabilities: Provides a structured framework for documenting domain expertise
Includes sections for core principles with examples
Defines step-by-step processes with inputs and outputs
Offers output templates for consistent deliverables
Includes intake questions to gather relevant context
author: ChaiWithJai
securitySummary: This is a pure markdown template file with no executable code, scripts, network calls, or file system access beyond its own location. The static analyzer produced 26 false positives by misinterpreting markdown syntax (backticks), standard Claude paths (~/.claude/), and documentation text as security threats. No actual cryptographic algorithms, external commands, or system reconnaissance code exists in this file.
valueStatement: Building domain expertise skills from scratch is challenging without a clear structure. This template provides a ready-made framework with principles, processes, and output templates so you can focus on your domain knowledge instead of format.
limitations: This is a template, not a ready-to-use skill for any specific domain
Requires manual customization with domain-specific content
Does not include any pre-built expertise for any domain
Users must test and iterate to ensure effectiveness

useCases.title: Document Specialized Knowledge
useCases.description: Capture and share your domain expertise in a structured format that others can follow.

useCases.title: Create Skill Frameworks
useCases.description: Build reusable templates for consistent skill documentation across your organization.

useCases.title: Develop Custom Skills
useCases.description: Create specialized skills tailored to specific industries or methodologies.

promptTemplates.title: Create New Skill
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new domain expertise skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the domain-expertise-template to create a skill for [your domain]. Include 3 core principles, a 3-step process, and at least one output template.

promptTemplates.title: Add Principles
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me define 3 core principles for my [domain] skill. For each principle, provide a clear explanation and good/bad examples.

promptTemplates.title: Define Process Steps
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a 3-step process for my [domain] skill using the template format. Include purpose, actions, and expected output for each step.

promptTemplates.title: Build Output Templates
promptTemplates.prompt: Write output templates for my [domain] skill. Include a markdown template with clear section headers and guidance for what to include in each section.

bestPractices: Replace all placeholders with specific, actionable content for your domain
Include real examples that demonstrate both good and bad approaches
Test the skill with actual use cases before publishing
antiPatterns: Leaving placeholder text like [PLACEHOLDER] in the final skill
Creating generic principles that apply to any domain
Skipping the testing phase and publishing an untuned skill

faq.question: What tools support this skill template?
faq.answer: This template works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Each tool interprets the structured sections and generates domain-specific outputs.

faq.question: How long does it take to customize a skill?
faq.answer: Basic customization takes 30-60 minutes. Adding detailed examples and testing may require 2-4 hours depending on domain complexity.

faq.question: Can I combine this with other skill templates?
faq.answer: Yes. You can reference or import sections from other skills. The template is designed to be modular and extensible.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this template?
faq.answer: This is a read-only template file. It does not access files, networks, or system resources. Your customization stays local.

faq.question: Why is my generated skill not triggering?
faq.answer: Check that the skill name and trigger conditions in the header are specific. Avoid generic names that match too many user requests.

faq.question: How is this different from a system prompt?
faq.answer: This template structures expertise into reusable sections. System prompts are single files. The template format makes complex expertise easier to maintain and extend.
